## SigMap commands

| When | Command |
|------|---------|
| Before answering a question about code | `sigmap ask "<your question>"` |
| After changing config or source dirs | `sigmap validate` |
| To verify an AI answer is grounded | `sigmap judge --response <file>` |

@dataclass Block(kind, text, heading_path, start_line, end_line)
@dataclass ChunkInfo(index, file, title, heading_path, start_line, end_line, chars, approx_tokens, blocks)
def approx_tokens(text: str) → int
def slugify(value: str, fallback: str) → str
def read_text(path: Path) → str
def split_front_matter(text: str) → tuple[str, str]
def block_kind(lines: list[str]) → str
def parse_blocks(markdown: str) → list[Block]
def split_large_block(block: Block, max_chars: int) → list[Block]
def make_overlap(blocks: list[Block], overlap_chars: int) → list[Block]
def chunk_blocks(blocks: list[Block], max_chars: int, min_chars: int, overlap_chars: int) → list[list[Block]]
def render_chunk(source: Path, chunk: list[Block], chunk_index: int, total_chunks: int, front_matter: str, include_front_matter: bool, add_header: bool) → str
def choose_title(chunk: list[Block], fallback: str) → str
def write_chunks(source: Path, out_dir: Path, max_chars: int, min_chars: int, overlap_chars: int, include_front_matter: bool, add_header: bool, dry_run: bool) → dict
def parse_args(argv: Iterable[str]) → argparse.Namespace
def main(argv: Iterable[str] | None) → int
